text: Stunting is one of the main public health problems in Indonesia that occurs due to children's physical growth deficiency under five years old and if so, it will lead to several long term negative impacts on their health and brain development. This study uses qualitative descriptive method to know about the stunting problem in the era of modernization. Although stunting rate in Indonesia is starting to decrease in recent years, the rate is still very high. Several factors are found to be contributing to the problem of stunting, including: 1) chronic malnutrition, 2) less than ideal childcare practices, 3) mothers who have insufficient knowledge of a balanced diet, 4) inadequate postnatal care, 5) high frequency of infections, and 4) poor sanitation. To overcome stunting in Indonesia requires a more complex approach that not only focused on the health sector, but also involved the other sectors. This paper proposed several solutions namely: 1) specific and sensitive nutrition intervention, 2) women empowerment, and 3) robust monitoring using technology. The stunting in Indonesia is required to be solved by an integrated effort from all related sectors to provide access to nutrition that enable all children to reach their full potential.
